The draft short description is factually accurate but omits the Dean Rutledge trade context, which is the key Sockeyes connection.

review.correctedDescriptionShort: Left winger Trevor Hendry, acquired from the Richmond Sockeyes in a trade for Dean Rutledge, scores five goals and adds three assists in his first two games with the Fort McMurray Oil Barons.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: The Fort McMurray Oil Barons acquired six-foot-two, 185-pound left winger Trevor Hendry from the BCJHL's Richmond Sockeyes in exchange for Dean Rutledge, the AJHL's third-leading scorer, who chose to finish his junior career in Richmond. In his first two home games against the Hobbema Hawks, Hendry scored five goals and added three assists while playing on a line with 16-year-old centre Brian Shantz and right winger Barry Kress. Hendry, a WHL veteran of three-and-a-half years, said the Barons organization had treated him well despite being far from home on the B.C. coast.
review.notes: No headline is visible in the OCR — the AI-generated headline should be cleared. The article is archive-worthy as it documents the Rutledge-for-Hendry trade directly involving the 1987 Sockeyes roster.
